I have OAuth accounts with both ChatGPT and Claude that work in web browsers.  I find that I want to programmatically issue questions to them.  Can you determine if this violates TOS?

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Check TOS for programmatic API access via OAuth

content.text:
Short answer: yes, almost certainly — for both services. Both companies route programmatic use to their paid APIs and prohibit automating the consumer web UIs.

**ChatGPT (OpenAI Consumer Terms / Usage Policies):** Prohibit "automated or programmatic" extraction/access to the Services, scraping, and circumventing rate limits or access controls. Browser-session scripting against chat.openai.com falls under this.

**Claude.ai (Anthropic Consumer Terms):** Prohibit accessing the Services through "automated means" (bots, scrapers) except where Anthropic has provided an interface for that purpose — which is the Anthropic API under separate Commercial Terms.

The supported paths:
- OpenAI: platform.openai.com API (separate billing from ChatGPT Plus/Pro)
- Anthropic: console.anthropic.com API (separate billing from Claude.ai)

Practical note: your existing OAuth subscription doesn't grant API access; the API is a distinct product with its own keys and pay-per-token billing. If cost is the concern, both offer cheap tiers (Haiku, GPT-4o-mini class) that often beat a Plus subscription for light scripted use.

Want me to pull the exact current TOS clauses from each so you have citations?

content.content:
# Automated Access Restrictions - Consumer Terms of Service

## Section 3: Use of Our Services

The Terms prohibit several forms of automated access:

**Restriction on automated access:**
"Except when you are accessing our Services via an Anthropic API Key or where we otherwise explicitly permit it, to access the Services through automated or non-human means, whether through a bot, script, or otherwise."

**Data harvesting prohibition:**
"To crawl, scrape, or otherwise harvest data or information from our Services other than as permitted under these Terms."

**General abuse clause:**
Users must not "abuse, harm, interfere with, or disrupt our Services, including, for example, introducing viruses or malware, spamming or DDoSing Services, or bypassing any of our systems or protective measures."

## Key Distinction: Consumer vs. Commercial

The preamble explicitly states: "Our Commercial Terms of Service govern your use of any Anthropic API key, the Anthropic Console, or any other Anthropic offerings that reference the Commercial Terms of Service. **For clarity, this does not include Claude.ai or Claude Pro use for individuals or entities.**"

This distinction means the automated access prohibition applies specifically to Claude.ai and Claude Pro consumer products, while different rules govern API usage under separate commercial terms.

Based on the search results, I found the relevant information about OpenAI's Terms of Use regarding automated or programmatic data extraction. Here's what the current terms state:

OpenAI's Terms of Use prohibit users from automatically or programmatically extracting data or Output. This restriction appears consistently across multiple versions of OpenAI's terms, including their standard Terms of Use and regional variations.

The specific language in the API terms states that users may not "except as permitted through the API, use any automated or programmatic method to extract data or output from the Services, including scraping, web harvesting, or web data extraction".

The search results show that OpenAI's Service Terms were last updated on January 9, 2026, and the current versions of these terms continue to include this restriction on automated data extraction as a prohibited activity.

input.query: OpenAI Terms of Use ChatGPT "you may not" "automated" scrape bot reverse engineer

content.content:
# OpenAI Terms of Use - Extracted Clauses

Based on the Hacker News post, here are the relevant clauses from OpenAI's terms:

**Programmatic Extraction:**
"Automatically or programmatically extract data or Output (defined below)."

**Competitive Use:**
"Use Output to develop models that compete with OpenAI."

**Reverse Engineering:**
"Attempt to or assist anyone to reverse engineer, decompile or discover the source code or underlying components of our Services, including our models, algorithms, or systems."

The post highlights tension between OpenAI's restrictions on automated access and their own training practices, which a commenter notes could undermine the enforceability of these terms.

What Terms of Use For Major Websites Say About Whether Generative AI Training Is Allowed On Their Content | Zuva","url":"https://zuva.ai/blog/llm-breach-of-terms-of-use/"},{"title":"Is it against ToS to create AutoHotKey script to automatize chatGPT's input? - ChatGPT - OpenAI Developer Community","url":"https://community.openai.com/t/is-it-against-tos-to-create-autohotkey-script-to-automatize-chatgpts-input/574281"},{"title":"CHATGPT scraping news site, UA and third parties - Plugins / Actions builders - OpenAI Developer Community","url":"https://community.openai.com/t/chatgpt-scraping-news-site-ua-and-third-parties/220504"},{"title":"Overview of OpenAI Crawlers","url":"https://platform.openai.com/docs/bots"}]

Based on the search results, here are the relevant restrictions from OpenAI's Terms of Use regarding the prohibitions you asked about:

## Automated/Bot Scraping

The Terms of Use prohibit automatically or programmatically extracting data or Output, and more specifically, users may not "except as permitted through the API, use any automated or programmatic method to extract data or output from the Services, including scraping, web harvesting, or web data extraction".

## Reverse Engineering

OpenAI prohibits users from attempting to or assisting anyone to reverse engineer, decompile or discover the source code or underlying components of OpenAI's Services, including its models, algorithms, or systems (except to the extent this restriction is prohibited by applicable law).

## Additional Restrictions

The Terms also include other related prohibitions: users may not extract data from the Services other than as permitted through the Services, and may not use Output to develop artificial intelligence models that compete with OpenAI's products and services.

These restrictions apply across OpenAI's various terms of use documents for different user types (consumers, business/developers, educators, etc.), though the specific wording may vary slightly between versions.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

aiTitle: Check TOS for programmatic API access via OAuth

content.text:
Here are the verbatim clauses for both services.

## Anthropic — Claude.ai Consumer Terms of Service (Section 3, "Use of Our Services")

> "Except when you are accessing our Services via an Anthropic API Key or where we otherwise explicitly permit it, [you must not access the Services] through automated or non-human means, whether through a bot, script, or otherwise."

> "[You must not] crawl, scrape, or otherwise harvest data or information from our Services other than as permitted under these Terms."

The preamble explicitly carves out the API: the Commercial Terms govern API/Console use; the Consumer Terms govern Claude.ai and Claude Pro. So your Claude Pro OAuth session is on the consumer side, and the automation prohibition applies.

## OpenAI — Terms of Use ("What you cannot do")

> "[You may not] except as permitted through the API, use any automated or programmatic method to extract data or output from the Services, including scraping, web harvesting, or web data extraction."

> "[You may not] automatically or programmatically extract data or Output."

> "[You may not] attempt to or assist anyone to reverse engineer, decompile or discover the source code or underlying components of our Services, including our models, algorithms, or systems."

Same pattern: the API is the permitted channel; the consumer ChatGPT session is not.

## Bottom line

Both TOS explicitly route programmatic access to the paid API and prohibit automating the consumer web session. If you want this without TOS risk:
- **Anthropic API** — console.anthropic.com (Haiku 4.5 is cheap for light scripted use)
- **OpenAI API** — platform.openai.com (gpt-4o-mini / similar tier)

Sources:
- [Anthropic Consumer Terms of Service](https://www.anthropic.com/legal/consumer-terms)
- [OpenAI Terms of Use (ROW)](https://openai.com/policies/row-terms-of-use/)
- [OpenAI Europe Terms of Use](https://openai.com/policies/eu-terms-of-use/)
- [OpenAI Terms & Policies index](https://openai.com/policies/)

content: You asked whether scripting your ChatGPT and Claude.ai OAuth sessions violates TOS; I confirmed both prohibit it and pulled the verbatim clauses. Next: decide whether to switch to the paid APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI).
